name: American General Bus Systems
recent_news: Listed at 2735 Signal Pkwy, Signal Hill, CA as 'American General Business Systems' — appears to be an office supply / business services retailer, not a school-bus operator. Likely SIC mis-coded in source list.
website: agbs.com (unverified; primary listings show as office-supply store)
fit_notes: Not a tuck-in target. Name appears in transport database but business listings (Yelp, Nextdoor) describe an office-supply/business-services company at the same address. SKIP.

name: Johannes Bus Co
principals: Harry 'Red' Johannes (founder, deceased); three generations of Johannes family operating
fleet_size_est: ~50-75 buses (small/full-size school buses, special-needs buses, charter coaches)
districts_or_customers: Contracts with 3 area school districts in the Quad Cities (Illinois/Iowa); serves Catholic schools historically; primary HQ Rock Island, IL with Galesburg, IL satellite location
recent_news: Celebrated 50 years in business (2022-2023). Family-owned, three generations active. No M&A activity reported.
fit_notes: Solid family-owned IL Quad Cities operator with 50+ year track record, 3 district contracts and 125+ employees. Multi-generational ownership = potential succession opportunity. Diversified school/SPED/charter mix; reasonable Midwest tuck-in candidate for Summit.

name: Reid School Bus Svc Inc
principals: J.J. Reid (Owner); operates as Reid & Reid, Inc.
founded_year: 1992 (incorporated in MD; family has 39+ years in transportation)
districts_or_customers: Calvert County Public Schools (primary); private charter trips throughout Southern Maryland
recent_news: No M&A activity. Long-tenured Southern Maryland family operator. Family describes 39+ years of providing transportation services in the community.
website: reidandreidinc.com (also reid-reidbuscharters.com); listed website 'chesapeake.net' is the local ISP, not the company
fit_notes: Small family-owned Calvert County, MD contractor (~13 buses). Sub-scale on its own but a clean bolt-on for a Mid-Atlantic platform. Long owner tenure suggests succession potential. Small for Summit unless used to seed Southern Maryland presence.

name: DS Bus Lines Inc
owner_or_parent: Beacon Mobility (portfolio company of Audax Group) — acquired from The Kincaid Group (TKG)
principals: Founded by Doerr/Schmiedeler families (1974 root business; DS Bus Lines branded 2011); now under Beacon Mobility management
founded_year: 2011 (DS Bus Lines as branded entity; parent contracting business dates to 1974)
fleet_size_est: 1,200+ vehicles (consolidated across DS Bus Lines, DS Bus South, STS New Mexico)
districts_or_customers: ~25 school district partners across 7 states (KS, MO, CO, TX, LA, NM, OK); includes Shawnee Mission KS, Columbia MO Public Schools, Chicago Public Schools (announced fall 2025); ~45,000 passengers daily
recent_news: Sold by The Kincaid Group to Beacon Mobility (Audax PE portfolio company) — major consolidation. Van Pool Transportation/Beacon STB acquisition of control approved by federal regulators (Aug 2022). Won CPS contract for fall 2025. Edwardsville, KS is an operating yard; HQ is Shawnee, KS.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target — DS Bus Lines is owned by Beacon Mobility (Audax Group PE), a direct platform competitor to Summit/I Squared. ~1,200 vehicles across 7 states. SKIP — direct competitor.

name: Keystone Bus Solutions LLC
districts_or_customers: Small businesses, nonprofits, and local governments in the Nashville/Middle Tennessee area — IT consulting / digital marketing customers, not school districts
recent_news: No school-bus activity. Company is Keystone Business Solutions (d/b/a Keystone / 'We Are Keystone'), a Nashville-area managed IT services, cybersecurity, and digital marketing firm. Same address (3050 Business Park Cir, Goodlettsville, TN), same phone (615-826-3500), and Mike Miller as principal confirms identity. Source list mis-categorized as 'Bus Solutions.'
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target — this is an IT/managed-services firm, not a school-bus operator. Likely mis-categorized in source data because of the word 'Bus(iness)' in name. SKIP.
